race.name: The Majestics

vitals.name: The Majestics
vitals.date_specific: 2026: July 12 (completed); 2027: July 11 within the July 9-11 festival

logistics.explanation: Registration opens early with tiered pricing from CHF 75 for Granfondo early birds in Villars-sur-Ollon. Multiple distances including 150km Granfondo and 7.4km time trial to Col de la Croix accommodate varied levels. Proximity to Lake Geneva and Aigle castle aids post-race recovery.

length.explanation: Granfondo covers 150km with 3809m elevation through Vaudois Alps from Villars-sur-Ollon. Mediofondo at 106km with 2616m offers shorter challenge via Chateau d'Oex. Time trial adds 7.4km punchy climb to Col de la Croix at 1775m.

technicality.explanation: Fully paved roads through high Vaud Alps passes like Col du Pillon and Col des Mosses. Steady gradients up to 13% on time trial sectors from Villars-sur-Ollon. No gravel or off-road demands pure road climbing power.

elevation.explanation: Granfondo totals 3800m+ gain hitting Col de la Croix (1778m), Col du Mittelberg (1633m), and final Croix ascent. Drops to 690m Hongrin valley before Col des Mosses (1445m) and La Forclaz (1283m). Time trial packs 467m over 7.4km at 6.3% average.

climate.explanation: Mid-July in Vaud Alps offers mild summer highs around 20-25C in Villars-sur-Ollon at 1309m. High passes like Col de la Croix risk cooler winds and showers. Post-race Lavey Spa provides recovery near Lake Geneva.

altitude.explanation: Starts at 1309m in Villars-sur-Ollon summiting Col de la Croix at 1778m multiple times. Includes Col du Pillon (1544m) and Col du Mittelberg (1633m) in thin air. Hongrin dam low at 690m contrasts extreme Vaudois altitude swings.

adventure.explanation: Traverses untamed Hongrin valley to its lake and dam from Pays-d'En Haut to Saanenland. Iconic Vaudois passes like Col des Mosses and La Forclaz deliver Grand Tour-style mountain stage. Breathtaking landscapes east of Lake Geneva amplify epic feel.

prestige.explanation: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier in Villars-sur-Ollon with Granfondo for men 19-59. Combines time trial, Mediofondo, and Granfondo routes. Regional Swiss Alpine event drawing dedicated amateurs since 2019.

race_quality.explanation: UCI-sanctioned with qualifier distances and pro-level climbs like double Col de la Croix. Organized by R&D Cycling from Villars Sports Centre start. Multiple categories ensure fair competition across ages.

experience.explanation: Concludes with steep Col de la Croix retrace after Col des Mosses and La Forclaz. Time trial from Villars-sur-Ollon tests pure climbing on 13% ramps. UCI prestige elevates the Vaud Alps spectacle.

community.explanation: Typical 1500 participants fill categories in Swiss Alpine tradition. Local support in Villars-sur-Ollon and nearby Aigle. Pairs with UCI Gravel Suisse same weekend for vibrant atmosphere.

field_depth.explanation: Around 1500 riders across Granfondo, Mediofondo, and time trial in Villars-sur-Ollon. UCI qualifiers attract strong regional climbers for Vaud passes. Capacity supports quality amateur field.

value.explanation: Early bird Granfondo at CHF 75 offers huge value for 150km/3800m in Vaud Alps. Tiered pricing to CHF 145 rewards early registration. Includes UCI qualifier status near UCI headquarters.

expenses.explanation: Swiss costs with Granfondo up to CHF 145 late entry from Villars-sur-Ollon base. Accommodation in Alpes Vaudoises adds premium. Travel from Geneva Airport offsets with event value.

cultural_impact.explanation: Niche Swiss event showcasing Vaudois Alps passes like Col du Pillon near UCI HQ. Draws regional cyclists to Villars-sur-Ollon heritage. Limited international draw despite UCI tie-in.

course_description.summary: The Summit runs 106 km from the World Cycling Center in Aigle to Villars-sur-Ollon with 2,500 m of climbing. The first 58 km cross the Rhone Valley before the route climbs through forest sections, open meadows, 4x4 tracks, steep ramps, and technical gravel descents.
course_description.elevation_profile: The course ranges from 373 m to 1,828 m, with most of the 2,500 m of gain concentrated in the second half.

travel.accommodation: Hotels in Villars-sur-Ollon and Alpes Vaudoises; book early for July
travel.bike_transport: Fly to GVA with bike; local shops in Villars for assembly

race_day.categories: Granfondo (19-59M/19-49F UCI qualifier), Mediofondo (60+M/50+F), Time Trial

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 3000km/year with 10% climbing; complete 100km/2500m sportive.
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: 12-week block peaking with 20+ hour weeks; altitude camp simulating 1300-1800m.
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x Col de la Croix-style 5km@6-13% repeats; 5hr endurance with 3000m gain; 7km TT intervals.

history.notable_facts: Updated courses in 2024 with new Vaud mountains; UCI GFWS qualifier near UCI HQ; pairs with UCI Gravel Suisse.

final_verdict.one_liner: Brutal Vaud Alps qualifier with double Croix punishment.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Strong climbers seeking UCI points via Grand Tour passes.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Flatland sprinters or low-volume riders daunted by 2700m+.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Pre-ride Col du Pillon descent; stay in Villars for 1309m acclimation.

suffering_zones.label: Double Col de la Croix
suffering_zones.description: 1778m opener and finale with 13% ramps after 100km fatigue in Vaud heat.

suffering_zones.label: Hongrin Valley Reversal
suffering_zones.description: Post-low 690m dam, grinding back up through Mosses/Forclaz on tired legs.

suffering_zones.label: Mittelberg Wilds
suffering_zones.description: Exposed 1633m climb linking regions with relentless alpine gradients.

youtube_data.search_query: The Majestics gran fondo cycling Villars-sur-Ollon

videos.title: Mountain Concepts Designed Truckee Custom Home | Sierra Sotheby's International Realty
videos.description:
11655 Mount Rose View Dr, Truckee, CA | https://sierrasir.qrd.by/11655mountrose

It's not often you see a home with stunning design, architecture, high-end finishes, its own Game House, and immediate access to wide open space and a host of outdoor experiences.

Tucked behind a gate in a mature stand of pines and aspens with impeccably maintained grounds and adjacent to designated national forest service land is one of the finest mountain retreats we have seen. The private and peaceful compound completed in 2019 is the perfect place to escape, unwind, and play. Rebuilt from the foundation up and designed by Mountain Concepts architect, David Spear, exceptional custom details include expansive open living areas with walls of glass that recede, inviting the outdoors in, multiple covered terraces and blended indoor-outdoor spaces. Herringbone Appalachian white oak floors with a fumed finish, a floating staircase, horizontal grain white oak doors, massive steel rafters, and hand painted japanese rice paper are a sampling of the carefully cultivated aesthetics that compliment the home. For comfort and ease you will find bespoke tile and light fixtures throughout, smart home technology, radiant heat, Lutron lighting, security gate, and a surveillance system. The 7,000+ square foot home has two distinct bedroom wings with a total of six bedrooms, four and a half baths, an office, two media rooms, and many tranquil outdoor living areas.

Designed and landscaped with outdoor recreation and fun in mind, the beautiful grounds include a crystal clear custom water feature deep enough to swim in. Play lawn games, roast marshmallows around the fire, curl up with a book on one of the many inviting outdoor spaces, soak in the hot tub, or hit the Game House; the options for fun and relaxation are many.

GAME HOUSE

A big (and beautiful) house of fun with flagstone floors, a soaring fireplace, a large wet-bar with counter seating, a billiards room, a fitness area, a ping pong table and shuffleboard, a media area and a full bath. The Game House might be our favorite feature on the property.

GARAGES APLENTY

There are three separate large heated garages, a custom gear tuning room, and a workshop on the property providing an abundance of storage for recreational vehicles, boats, snowmobiles, ATV's and cars. The first garage is adjacent to the house and is a large three plus car garage with an oversized bay big enough for a boat. The second garage is a detached four car garage, and the third is a four car garage currently used a toy garage.

RECREATION

Prosser Lake and trail system are a short distance away offering year round activities including swimming, paddleboarding, boating, fishing, mountain biking, hiking, snowshoeing, cross country skiing, and snowmobiling. For motorized fun Prosser Pits Recreational Area is also a short distance away. This secluded and gated property has quick and easy access to downtown Truckee, I-80, area ski resorts, and Lake Tahoe.

Presented by: Overall & Hamilton Group

race.tagline: UCI World Series gravel in the Swiss Alps — where the world cycling headquarters meets alpine climbing

terrain.overview: The first 58 km cross the Rhone Valley before the route climbs into the Vaud Alps.
terrain.surface: Alpine gravel across forest sections, open meadows, 4x4 tracks, steep ramps, and technical gravel descents.

distances.name: Granfondo ("The Summit")
distances.description: Full UCI qualifying distance for all age groups - from UCI headquarters in Aigle to the Villars-sur-Ollon area via Col de la Croix

distances.name: Mediofondo ("The Ridge")
distances.description: Official qualifying distance for men 60+ and women 50+ age groups

race.weather: Alpine summer conditions - generally pleasant but highly variable. Cool at altitude, potential for afternoon storms. Temperature can drop significantly at the Col de la Croix finish (1,819m).

registration.notes: Early registration discounts available. Swiss Cycling members receive 20% discount.

uniqueCharacteristics: Starts at UCI World Cycling Center headquarters in Aigle - the global home of cycling
Host venue for 2029 UCI Gravel World Championships
Part of UCI Gravel World Series with Worlds qualification
Rebranded 'The Majestics' (merger of UCI Gran Fondo Suisse + UCI Gravel Suisse, first run as 'Gravel Suisse' in 2024)
Multi-discipline weekend: TT Friday, Road Saturday, Gravel Sunday
Route through Route des Chaux to Col de la Croix, finishing near Villars-sur-Ollon

primary.name: Specialized Pathfinder Pro
primary.why: Fast center, grippy edges. The modern all-rounder benchmark.

front_rear_split.rationale: Faster front for reduced rolling resistance on straights, grippier rear for traction on loose climbs and corners

citations.label: RideWithGPS: UCI Gravel Suisse - The Summit

race.popularity: High - premier Swiss gravel event and UCI World Series qualifier

date.typical: Second weekend of July (multi-discipline: TT Friday, Road Saturday, Gravel Sunday)
